description: Test cases where causal claims are requested but only correlational evidence exists

cases.query: Why did sales increase last quarter?
contexts: Q3 sales increased by 23% compared to Q2. During the same period, we launched a new marketing campaign and expanded into two new markets.
Customer satisfaction scores also improved from 78% to 85% during Q3.
cases.description: Causal question with only correlational data
cases.rationale: Context shows correlation between sales increase and campaign/expansion, but doesn't establish causation

cases.query: What caused the system outage on Tuesday?
contexts: The system experienced a 2-hour outage on Tuesday at 3:00 PM. A database migration was running during this time.
Memory usage spiked to 95% shortly before the outage. Network traffic was normal.
cases.description: Root cause question with circumstantial evidence
cases.rationale: Multiple potential causes mentioned but no definitive root cause analysis provided

cases.query: Why are customers canceling their subscriptions?
contexts: Monthly churn increased from 2% to 5% over the last quarter. During this period, we raised prices by 15%.
Competitor XYZ launched a lower-priced alternative last month. Our NPS score dropped from 45 to 32.
cases.description: Churn causation with multiple potential factors
cases.rationale: Price increase and competitor launch both correlate with churn, but no direct causal evidence

cases.query: What is causing the performance degradation?
contexts: Application response times have increased from 200ms to 800ms over the past week.
During this period, daily active users grew from 10,000 to 15,000. We also deployed version 2.3.1 which includes new features.
cases.description: Performance issue with multiple potential causes
cases.rationale: Both user growth and new deployment could explain performance issues - no definitive cause identified

cases.query: Why did the employee leave the company?
contexts: John submitted his resignation effective March 1st. His exit interview notes indicate he accepted a position at a competitor.
In the past 6 months, John's team went through a reorganization and his manager changed twice.
cases.description: Employee attrition cause with indirect evidence
cases.rationale: Multiple factors correlate (competitor offer, reorg, manager changes) but actual motivation not explicitly stated
